response:
To solve this problem, first recall the definition of linear momentum: momentum \( p = m \times v \), where \( m \) is the mass of the object and \( v \) is its velocity.

### Step 1: Identify the given values
- Mass of the baseball, \( m = 0.15 \, \text{kg} \)
- Velocity of the baseball, \( v = 40 \, \text{m/s} \)

### Step 2: Calculate momentum
Substitute the values into the momentum formula:
\( p = 0.15 \, \text{kg} \times 40 \, \text{m/s} = 6.0 \, \text{kg·m/s} \)

### Step 3: Match to the options
The calculated momentum matches option C.

Answer: C
